name: São Tomé and Príncipe
avoid: October through May is the wetter stretch, so south-coast road days, Pico de São Tomé trail conditions, and small-boat departures to Ilhéu das Rolas are all less predictable than in the June-September gravana season.
Príncipe flights are the main bottleneck: seats are limited, schedules can shift, and missing the day's flight can easily force an extra night on São Tomé.
Card acceptance is inconsistent outside top hotels, so keep STN cash for taxis, moto rides, plantation visits, and pier-side boat payments.
stopoverNote: If you only have 5-6 days, stay on São Tomé island and split the trip between the capital and a south-coast eco-lodge instead of forcing the expensive Príncipe flight.

cities.name: São Tomé City
cities.notes: The capital is the practical landing base for cash, SIMs, market walks, the fort, and the easiest day loop to Boca do Inferno and the north-coast roças.

cities.name: Príncipe
cities.notes: Príncipe is the quieter, pricier second island for Praia Banana, Roça heritage, and low-density beach-and-biosphere stays around Santo António.

cities.name: Obo National Park
cities.notes: Use the south and central forest belt for roça lunches, Ilhéu das Rolas boat days, and the serious guided hiking logistics around São Tomé's rainforest core.

itinerary.sim: CST and Unitel STP prepaid SIMs are easiest to sort in São Tomé city; buy enough data before leaving the capital because signal is patchier on the far south coast and on parts of Príncipe.

São Tomé City.name: São Tomé City
São Tomé City.note: Give the capital two nights for arrival admin, an easy old-town day, and one north-coast outing before you move into the slower south-coast or inter-island section.

days.theme: Arrival, Fort Views and the Capital Waterfront

activities.name: Taxi from Nuno Xavier Airport to central São Tomé or the Omali area
activities.tip: About 50-100 STN is a normal short transfer; agree the fare before loading bags because meters are rarely used.

activities.name: São Sebastião Fort exterior and Ana Chaves bay walk
Mercado Municipal de São Tomé browse
Motorbike taxi hop between the fort, market and seafront
activities.tip: Short moto rides are cheap and useful in the heat, but settle the price first and carry small cash.

days.theme: Boca do Inferno and the North-Coast Plantation Loop

activities.name: Half-day taxi loop to Boca do Inferno and the north-coast roças
activities.tip: A private car is the easiest way to combine the blowhole with plantation stops without waiting for shared transport.

activities.name: Boca do Inferno blowhole stop
activities.tip: There is no formal ticket for independent visitors; go with calm footing because the black lava rock gets slippery.

activities.name: Roça Agostinho Neto community visit
activities.tip: Recent visitors commonly report an informal local contribution of about €20 per person rather than a fixed museum-style ticket.

activities.name: Roça Monte Café coffee and plantation visit
activities.tip: Guided coffee-and-roça day tours usually start around €105-140 with transport and lunch, so it is best value if you want context rather than just a quick photo stop.

Obo National Park.name: Obo National Park
Obo National Park.note: The park works best from a south-coast eco-lodge or Angolares base: one cultural day, one Ilhéu das Rolas boat day, and one full rainforest day.

days.theme: South-Coast Transfer, Roça Lunch and Black-Sand Beaches

activities.name: Private taxi from São Tomé City to Angolares or Praia Inhame
activities.tip: Budget more if you want scenic stops on the east coast because there is no reliable meter or fixed tariff board.

activities.name: Roça São João dos Angolares plantation lunch and estate visit
activities.tip: Chef João Carlos Silva's roça is one of the country's best-known food stops; reserve ahead because lunch service is destination dining, not a drop-in café.

activities.name: Praia Jalé or Praia Piscina beach stop

days.theme: Ilhéu das Rolas Equator Marker and South-Coast Snorkeling

activities.name: Guided boat day to Ilhéu das Rolas with equator stop and snorkeling
activities.tip: That matches the common €75-105 full-day excursion band from Praia Inhame or Porto Alegre, usually including the boat and often lunch.

activities.name: Motorbike or short taxi transfer to Porto Alegre pier if not bundled
Equator monument and Praia Bateria swim on Ilhéu das Rolas

days.theme: Ôbo Rainforest Logistics and the Pico de São Tomé Climb

activities.name: Ôbo National Park access and conservation area
activities.tip: The park itself does not usually charge an entrance ticket; the real cost is your guide, transport and any camping gear.

activities.name: Guided Pico de São Tomé day hike from Bom Sucesso
activities.tip: Current guided day hikes are typically priced from about €140 with transport and a picnic; book only if you are fit for a long, muddy 8-11 hour summit day.

activities.name: Alternative Lagoa Amélia forest walk if summit weather closes in
activities.tip: Ask your operator to downgrade instead of cancel if the high ridge is too wet; you still get rainforest exposure with lower risk.

Príncipe.name: Príncipe
Príncipe.note: Príncipe is best kept to two or three nights because flights are expensive; use that time for one beach-and-view day and one heritage-and-biosphere day.

days.theme: Flight to Príncipe, Santo António and Praia Banana

activities.name: Road transfer back to São Tomé airport from the south coast
STP Airways flight from São Tomé to Príncipe
activities.tip: Book early and reconfirm the day before because seats are limited and timing changes are one of the biggest itinerary risks in the country.

activities.name: Taxi from Príncipe Airport to Santo António, Bom Bom or Sundy
Praia Banana and Roça Belo Monte viewpoint stop

days.theme: Roça Heritage, Santo António and a Slow North-Coast Day

activities.name: Taxi or hotel 4x4 for Santo António, Roça Sundy and north-coast viewpoints
activities.tip: Private transport is practical because public options on Príncipe are limited and spread out.

activities.name: Santo António old-town walk
Roça Sundy heritage visit and eclipse-history stop
activities.tip: Prices vary by whether you join a guided estate visit through your hotel or just pay a small local access fee at the property.

activities.name: Bom Bom islet boardwalk and swim

connections.method: Private taxi or arranged hotel transfer down the east or south coast toward Angolares, Porto Alegre or Praia Inhame.
Return road transfer to São Tomé airport, then STP Airways inter-island flight and a short taxi onward on Príncipe.

extras: June through September is the most reliable gravana window for this route, with drier hiking conditions and better odds for calm small-boat departures to Ilhéu das Rolas.
Príncipe is stunning but expensive because the domestic flight dominates the budget; if the forecast looks poor, keep more days on São Tomé instead of forcing the flight.
For Pico de São Tomé, treat boots, rain protection and a guide as mandatory rather than optional upgrades because the trail can get slick and visibility can drop quickly.
Moto taxis are useful for short hops in the capital and around pier areas, but for luggage days or plantation loops, standard taxis and pre-arranged drivers are more realistic.
Roça lunches and estate visits often require advance notice, especially at São João dos Angolares, so lock those in before you leave the capital.

Obo National Park: Obo National Park Sao Tome rainforest
